title: AI search grounded in Facebook posts? What could go wrong?
slides: Meta's AI searches your old Facebook posts. Should you worry?
It indexes private data you thought was forgotten.
But this data reveals more than you intended to share.
Your future job interviews could be trained on your past.
Review your privacy settings today before it's too late.
thread_text:
1. Meta is watching your past.

Meta’s AI scans every old post and photo you uploaded—even if you deleted the app.

2. Forgotten data, fresh risks.

That 2014 status about your weekend habits now trains models that infer your politics, health, and income.

3. Your past predicts your future.

Potential employers already use AI tools—now they’ll feed them your own teen years’ posts.

4. You’re training your own judge.

The same AI that scored your college photos now decides loan rates and insurance premiums.

5. Lock your digital attic now.

Open Facebook’s privacy checker today. Remove old posts tagged with location, emotion, or job history.
video_script: Meta mines your deleted posts now.
Old statuses reveal more than photos.
Your past trains hiring algorithms today.
Loan AI learns from your teen.

script: Meta mines your deleted posts now.
Old statuses reveal more than photos.
Your past trains hiring algorithms today.
Loan AI learns from your teen.

instagram.caption:
That drunk photo from 2014 just became training data for loan algorithms. 🧠

Meta’s AI now indexes every old post, deleted app data, and forgotten status you thought was private. It uses this to infer your politics, health, and income.

Employers can already feed your teen years into hiring AI—scoring your past against your future. 🎯

Your old posts don’t just haunt you socially; they set interest rates and insurance premiums.

When did you last check what your digital attic reveals about you? ⏰

carousel_slides.description: Meta scans old posts and photos you deleted, uncovering forgotten data now used to model your life.
Habit posts now infer your politics, health, and income, creating risks from forgotten data.
Employers use AI tools trained on your own past posts to decide your future job interviews.
College photos train AI that now sets your loan rates, insurance premiums, and more.
